content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 19, 2017


Brittany E. Roberto
Deputy City Attorney
Costa Mesa City Attorney's Office
Jones & Mayer
3777 North Harbor Blvd.
Fullerton, CA 92835

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-17-216

Dear Ms. Roberto:

       This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the mass mailing prohibition of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the
provisions of the Act and not under other general ethics laws outside the Act.

       Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying this question should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

QUESTION

Is distribution of a mailing by the city containing the official photograph of all members of
the City Council prohibited under Section 89001 and Regulation 18901?

CONCLUSION

The mailing would be prohibited by Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 as discussed
below.

FACTS

       Each month, the City of Costa Mesa mails approximately 1,000 business license renewal
letters to businesses throughout the City. With the renewal letters, the City attaches an insert
relating to the Costa Mesa Chamber of Commerce, encouraging businesses to join the Chamber

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-17-216
Page No. 2

of Commerce. On one side of the insert, the City would like to include a welcome message from
the Costa Mesa City Council. The City would like the welcome message to contain a photograph
of all five members of the City Council. The photograph is the official photograph of the City
Council that is featured on the City's website. No Council Member is featured more prominently
than any other Council Member.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 provides that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense." Regulation 18901 interprets this provision. In pertinent part, Regulation 18901 provides
four criteria for what is considered a "mass mailing."

"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of
this subdivision (a)(1), the item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written document.

"(2) The item sent either:

"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated[2] with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or

"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an
elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing,
and is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with
the elected officer.

"(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys; or

"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $ 50.00 are paid
with public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent
of sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation.

"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b)."

Your question solely relates to the third criterion. As noted above, there are two alternate
circumstances where a mailing meets this criterion. The most applicable to your question is
Regulation 18901(a)(2)(B). Regulation 18901(a)(2)(B) provides that a mailing is prohibited
(assuming the other three criteria are met) if it includes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer. Since

2 "'Elected officer affiliated with the agency' means an elected officer who is a member, officer, or employee
of the agency, or of a subunit thereof such as a committee, or who has supervisory control over the agency, or who
appoints one or more members of the agency." (Regulation 18901(c)(1).)

File No. A-17-216
Page No. 3

the City will be inserting a photograph of the city councilmembers this factor is met. The fact that
the elected official in the photograph is part of a group would not change this result. (Fisicaro
Advice Letter, No. A-96-230.)

Even if it was the case that the individual councilmembers did not cooperate, consult,
coordinate, or act in concert with city staff in producing that mailing, as required by Regulation
18901(a)(2)(B), the standard in Regulation 18901(a)(2)(A) would also be met. The
Councilmembers are affiliated with the sender of the mailing and they are featured because it
includes their photograph. Regulation 18901(c)(2) provides: "'Features an elected officer' means
that the item mailed includes the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the elected
officer by the manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of the document, such as
by headlines, captions, type size, typeface, or type color." [Emphasis added.]

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Jack C. Woodside
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

sections.question:
Is distribution of a mailing by the city containing the official photograph of all members of 
the City Council prohibited under Section 8900 I and Regulation 18901?
sections.conclusion:
The mailing would be prohibited by Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 as discussed 
below.
sections.facts:

letters to businesses throughout the City. With the renewal letters, the City attaches an insert 
relating to the Costa Mesa Chamber of Commerce, encouraging businesses to join the Chamberof Commerce. On one side of the insert, the City would like to include a welcome message from 
the Costa Mesa City Council. The City would like the welcome message to contain a photograph 
of all five members of the City Council. The photograph is the official photograph of the City 
Council that is featured on the City's website. No Council Member is featured more prominently 
than any other Council Member.

the City will be inserting a photograph of the city councilmembers this factor is met. The fact that

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is distribution of a mailing by the city containing the official photograph of all members of 
the City Council prohibited under Section 8900 I and Regulation 18901?

CONCLUSION: The mailing would be prohibited by Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 as discussed 
below.
